curation.synopsis: Commander Marcus Valerius of the Therion Cohort marches alongside the Raven Guard, a soldier of the new Imperium who has always scorned the whispered cult of the Lectitio Divinitatus. Then a sudden flash of certainty — call it instinct, call it prayer — pulls his column off a road that would have been their grave. As Valerius questions whether the vision was a guiding hand or only his own frightened mind, the first quiet seeds of what will one day become the Imperial Creed are sown on a war-torn field. A pocket-sized meditation on faith, doubt, and the difference between hearing the divine and inventing it.
